title: iam-jit admin-action audit event (OCSF-shaped)
description:
Constrained subset of the OCSF v1.1.0 class 6003 audit-event for admin-action records (`unmapped.iam_jit.event_type == "ADMIN_ACTION"`). Every operator mutation (profile install/swap, rule add/remove, preset apply, pause start/stop, license install, backup create/restore, diagnostics bundle, config import/export, session kill, alert-rule edit, ...) lands in the audit channel as an OCSF event that ALSO conforms to this admin-action shape so a SIEM dashboard can filter `unmapped.iam_jit.event_type:"ADMIN_ACTION"` and get the operator-mutation feed in isolation.

This schema is `allOf` ocsf-iam-jit-audit-event.schema.json + the admin-action-specific constraints. The base OCSF schema MUST also pass. Per [[security-team-audit-export]] Lens B: admin actions are first-class in the audit stream.

Authoritative builders: src/iam_jit/bouncer/audit_export/admin_action.py (ibounce), internal/audit/admin_action.go (kbounce, dbounce when added).

status_id.description: ADMIN_ACTION events always Success — the mutation has already landed by the time the event is built.

event_type.description: Discriminator for filtering admin-action records out of the broader audit stream.

kind.description: What kind of admin action fired. Documented values cross-product: profile.install, profile.swap, profile.assign, profile.delete, rule.add, rule.remove, pause.start, pause.stop, preset.apply, session.kill, config.import, config.export, license.install, alert-rule.edit, diagnostics.bundle, backup.create, backup.restore. Unknown values are accepted (per [[deliberate-feature-completion]]: never refuse an audit row over a vocabulary mismatch).

source.description: Where the action originated. cli=operator-typed CLI command; mcp=MCP-tool-invoked; api=admin HTTP API; unknown=fallback for source-resolution failures.

actor.description: Operator identity (email, username, OIDC sub). Resolved via resolve_operator() when not supplied by the caller.

target.description: The entity the action mutated. Empty when the action has no target (e.g. license.install).

kind.description: profile | rule | pause_window | preset | task | license | backup | bundle | session

id.description: Human-readable identifier (profile name, rule pattern+id, pause id, backup file basename, ...).

extra.description: Per-target fields (profile source URL, rule effect, pause duration_seconds). Token-leak keys (token, password, secret, api_key, ...) are stripped before landing on the wire.

before_hash.description: Optional sha256 of the pre-mutation state. The full snapshot NEVER lands on the wire (could be arbitrarily large + could contain secrets); only the 64-hex digest does.

after_hash.description: Optional sha256 of the post-mutation state. Same discipline as before_hash.

extra.description: Optional per-event context. Token-leak keys are stripped before landing on the wire.
